compiled_state.title: Write RED unit tests for svg_gen: wireframe SVG regeneration from spatial-layout.json
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## What
Write failing pytest unit tests for the svg_gen submodule before implementation.

## testing_mode: RED — new source file.

## SVG format reference
See plugins/dso/skills/design-wireframe/docs/output-format-reference.md §Required SVG Structure.
Key: <svg viewBox='0 0 1440 900'>, component <g id='component-id'>, <rect> for bounds, tag-new class for NEW components.

## Test Cases (Given/When/Then)
- SG-1: Given a spatial-layout.json with one component having spatial_hint bounds, when generate_svg() runs, then output is valid XML with an <svg> root element
- SG-2: Given a component with id='comp-1' and spatial_hint bounds, when generate_svg() runs, then output contains <g id='comp-1'>
- SG-3: Given a component with tag=NEW, when generate_svg() runs, then its <g> element has class='tag-new'
- SG-4: Given a component with tag=MODIFIED, when generate_svg() runs, then its <g> element has class='tag-modified'
- SG-5: Given components with parent-child nesting, when generate_svg() runs, then child components are nested inside parent <g> elements
- SG-6: Given a spatial-layout.json where a component was removed (not present), when generate_svg() runs, then no <g> element with the removed component's id appears in the output
- SG-7: Given the output SVG, when cross-referenced with spatial-layout.json component IDs, then every component ID in the JSON has a corresponding <g id=...> in the SVG

## Files to Create
- tests/unit/test_figma_merge_svg.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/unit/test_figma_merge_svg.py exists with SG-1 through SG-7
  Verify: for t in SG-1 SG-2 SG-3 SG-4 SG-5 SG-6 SG-7; do grep -q "$t" tests/unit/test_figma_merge_svg.py || exit 1; done
- [ ] pytest exits non-zero (RED)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/unit/test_figma_merge_svg.py; [ $? -ne 0 ]
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
